---
title: "TidyTuesday Challenge"
format: html
---



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(scales)
```
:::




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Using R
# Option 1: tidytuesdayR R package 
## install.packages("tidytuesdayR")

tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2026-02-24')
## OR
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load(2026, week = 8)

sfi_grants <- tuesdata$sfi_grants

# Option 2: Read directly from GitHub

sfi_grants <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2026/2026-02-24/sfi_grants.csv')
```
:::


Cleaning 


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot_data <- sfi_grants %>%
  group_by(programme_name) %>%
  summarise(total_commitment = sum(current_total_commitment, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(total_commitment, n = 20)
```
:::





::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
ggplot(plot_data, aes(y = reorder(programme_name, total_commitment), x = total_commitment, 
  fill = total_commitment)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_viridis_c() +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = label_dollar(prefix = "€", scale_cut = cut_short_scale())) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 Research Bodies by Total Funding",
    subtitle = "Based on current total commitment across all programs",
    x = "Total Investment (EUR)",
    y = "Research Institution",
    fill = "Total Funding") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 7))
```
